ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for baked laksa pasta specifically; closest parent 'pasta dish' (FOODON_00002473) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for baked laksa-flavoured pasta with lion's mane mushroom.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for this fusion baked pasta dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of baked pasta laksa is approximately 380 g, comprising a full baked pasta portion topped with melted cheese crust, served in a single oven-proof dish.

nutrient_source.title: Laksa (Singapore) and Pasta (cooked) composite estimate — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy + FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for baked pasta laksa with lion's mane mushroom in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by component-weighted composition: cooked pasta (~55% by weight, USDA SR Legacy #20121), laksa broth with coconut milk (~25%, FAO/INFOODS SEA laksa analogue), melted cheese topping (~10%, USDA SR Legacy cheddar #01009), lion's mane mushroom (~10%, USDA FoodData Central #2103695). Energy and macros are blended proportionally. All -1 sentinel values indicate nutrients not reported across the composite source set.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — pasta (cooked, white) analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for baked pasta laksa with lion's mane mushroom. GI of 52 is estimated from cooked white pasta (GI ~47–54, mean ~52) per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. The laksa coconut-milk sauce and cheese topping are low-GI components that do not materially raise the overall GI above the pasta baseline. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 74.1) / 100 ≈ 38.5, where carbohydrate per 380 g serving = 19.5 × 3.8 = 74.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from laksa paste, shrimp paste (belacan), and cheese
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g (≈1596 mg per 380 g serving), this dish provides more than two-thirds of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-salt preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 52 (low) but a high glycemic load of ~38.5 per full serving due to the large pasta portion, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion (≈190 g) to limit postprandial glucose excursion, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to slow gastric emptying.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk and cheese, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 4.8 g saturated fat per 100 g (primarily from coconut milk and melted cheese) and high sodium (~420 mg/100 g) warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption in reduced portions is advisable, and the dish should not be a daily dietary staple.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked Pasta Laksa with Lion's Mane Mushroom is a Singaporean fusion dish that combines the bold, aromatic flavours of traditional laksa — a spiced coconut-milk broth made with lemongrass, galangal, chilli, and shrimp paste — with cooked pasta instead of the conventional rice noodles. The dish is assembled by tossing the pasta in a rich laksa sauce enriched with coconut milk, then topping it with lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) and a generous layer of cheese before baking in an oven until the cheese forms a golden, bubbling crust. The result is a dish that marries the creamy, spicy, and umami-laden character of Singaporean laksa with the comforting texture of baked pasta au gratin. The lion's mane mushroom contributes a mild, seafood-like flavour and a meaty, fibrous texture, while also providing dietary fibre and bioactive compounds. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 178 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~7.2 g/100 g) from cheese and mushroom, significant saturated fat (~4.8 g/100 g) from coconut milk and cheese, and a moderate carbohydrate load (~19.5 g/100 g) from the pasta. Sodium is notably high (~420 mg/100 g) due to the laksa paste and shrimp paste base, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), consistent with cooked pasta, though the glycemic load per full serving is substantial. This dish is served at Flavours by Saute at Funan Mall, Singapore, and is part of a baked pasta series requiring extended preparation time.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific fusion dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of approved USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA component entries. GI is estimated by analogy from cooked pasta per the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are well-established and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the composite source set for this dish type. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the composite source set. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the composite source set. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc are estimated from component sources and carry moderate uncertainty. The dish contains shrimp paste (belacan) and is therefore not halal-certified by default; halal status should be verified with the restaurant. Lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) is the featured functional ingredient. The restaurant is Flavours by Saute at Funan Mall, Singapore. Price SGD 19.80. field survey source URL preserved from input for provenance tracing only; not used as a nutritional source.
